data.title: Completion verifier: story 2932-51a6 closed with 5 open child tasks whose acceptance criteria were not met
data.description:
## Problem

Story 2932-51a6 (dso:ui-designer agent) was closed by the sprint orchestrator despite having 5 open child tasks (ab85-baf6, faa0-cb2c, f02d-e3dc, 08a3-cae0, 79b0-ce0b) with acceptance criteria that were not fully met at closure time.

The dso:completion-verifier agent (dispatched at sprint Step 10a for story closure) either:
1. Did not check whether all child tasks were closed before approving story closure
2. Did not verify that each task's acceptance criteria were actually satisfied
3. Was not dispatched at all for this story

## Expected Behavior

The completion verifier (CLAUDE.md Never-Do rule 24) must be dispatched at story closure and should:
1. Verify all child tasks are in closed status
2. Spot-check acceptance criteria of closed tasks against actual codebase state
3. Block story closure if open children or unmet criteria are found

## Actual Behavior

Story closed with 5 open children. Post-mortem investigation found:
- 3 tasks were met or superseded by alternative implementations (different file names/structure than specified)
- 2 tasks were genuinely not met (design_creation routing entry missing, EXPECTED_CATEGORIES drift unfixed)

## Scope

Investigate the completion-verifier agent definition (plugins/dso/agents/completion-verifier.md) and sprint SKILL.md Step 10a to determine:
1. Whether the verifier checks child task status as part of story validation
2. Whether it verifies acceptance criteria against the actual codebase (not just task status)
3. Whether it was dispatched for story 2932-51a6 or skipped

Related: bug 2fd0-5491 (ticket system allows closing stories with open children)
